verdict_reasoning: BAX is a canonical pro-apoptotic BCL2-family member with primary residence in the cytosol and outer mitochondrial membrane. However, during apoptosis and other forms of programmed cell death, BAX translocates to the plasma membrane as part of the immunogenic cell-death response — externalized on the outer leaflet during apoptotic membrane blebbing and phosphatidylserine exposure. This cell-death-induced surface display qualifies as a documented non-baseline context. BAX also engages mitochondrial and ER membranes during apoptosis, but the PM translocation during cell death is the surface-accessibility signal.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and stability of BAX PM exposure during apoptosis—whether it constitutes a therapeutically-targetable surface pool or is transient/secondary to primary mitochondrial-membrane perturbation. No documented baseline surface pool outside of cell-death context.
